title: nexus.deploy.yaml
description: Deployment manifest for the nexus Go framework. Declares the contract between a binary and its orchestration platform: deployments, peers, environments, inputs, and per-environment overrides.

deployments.description: Topology declaration: every deployment unit, what modules it owns, its listening port, and per-unit listener overrides. `nexus build --deployment X` reads this to generate zz_deploy_gen.go for X.

owns.description: Modules this deployment compiles locally. Modules not listed are HTTP-stub shadows. Omit entirely (or use null) for monolith (owns everything).

port.description: TCP port this deployment listens on. Baked into Config.Addr at build time.

prefix.description: Route prefix prepended to every user-mounted route (REST + GraphQL + WS). Framework routes (/__nexus, /health) stay unprefixed.

listeners.description: Additional bound listeners beyond the public port. Each entry needs `scope:` (public / admin / internal); addr auto-derives (admin = port+1000, internal = port+2000).

scope.description: Visibility scope. public = external traffic, admin = dashboard + introspection, internal = peer-to-peer + health probes.

addr.description: Bind address. Optional — auto-derived from port + offset when omitted.

peers.description: Per-peer transport settings consulted by codegen'd remote-service clients. Keyed by DeployAs tag.

url.description: Single-replica peer URL. Mutually exclusive with `urls`.

urls.description: Multi-replica URLs (round-robin + eject). Mutually exclusive with `url`.

retries.description: Retry count for transient failures (5xx + network errors). 0 disables.

min_version.description: Minimum peer version this client tolerates. Reads from peer's /__nexus/config.

token.description: Bearer token. Use ${VAR} for env interpolation; never commit literal secrets.

environments.description: Declared deploy targets (production, staging, preview, ...). The platform consults this to know which environments the binary supports; Config.Environment / NEXUS_ENVIRONMENT selects one at boot.

domain.description: Routing target — the platform attaches inbound traffic for this environment to this hostname.

autoscale.description: Replica count bounds. Platform decides the metric + policy.

ttl.description: Ephemeral environment lifetime. Non-empty marks the env as auto-destroyed (typical for per-PR preview deploys).

secrets.description: Sensitive inputs the operator supplies via the platform's encrypted secret store. Distinct from `env` so the platform UI can render with redaction + rotation hints.

description.description: Human-readable explanation for the platform UI.

required.description: When true, boot refuses to start if the secret is unset. Default false.

env_scoped.description: True = different value per environment (typical for API keys). False = one value shared across all envs.

rotate_alert.description: Platform reminder cadence — emit a warning once the secret has not been written for this long.

files.description: Mounted blobs the platform writes to known paths (TLS bundles, JSON config overrides, etc.).

path.description: Mount path inside the container. Must be absolute.

secret.description: True = encrypted at rest, redacted in any UI rendering of the file's contents.

env_scoped.description: True = each environment gets its own file content.

schema.description: JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) the file's contents must satisfy. Platform validates pre-deploy and rejects override violations.

hooks.description: Platform-orchestrated commands run outside the binary at well-defined deploy phases. Distinct from in-binary StartupTasks (which run inside the process at fx.Start).

build.description: Runs on the platform's build node before the image is finalized. Typical: `go build`, `npm run build`.

predeploy.description: Runs after the image is built and before traffic shifts. Typical: schema migrations, cache warmups.

postdeploy.description: Runs after the new release is live. Typical: CDN purges, Slack notifications, smoke tests.

environment_overrides.description: Per-environment diffs applied to the base inputs at merge time. Keys must match a declared environment. Cannot introduce inputs not in the base — only adjust existing ones.

env.description: Scalar locks: replace the effective default and mark the field read-only for operators in this environment.

env_specs.description: Spec diffs: field-by-field merge into the base env declaration (default, required, validation, etc.).

secret_specs.description: Spec diffs for secrets. Same merge rules as env_specs.

services.description: Service patches. Deep-merge into the base service declaration (size, backup, expose_as additions).

removed.description: Dotted paths to drop from this environment's effective manifest. Bucket must be env|secrets|files|services|volumes.

services.description: Backing services to provision (DB, cache, queue). The platform fulfills each by binding a managed sidecar and injecting connection env vars via expose_as.

expose_as.description: Map logical service fields to env-var names the app reads. Well-known keys: url, host, port, user, password, database, vhost, exchange.

env.description: Env vars the app reads, with required/secret flags + bound_to pointers. Auto-populated by `nexus reconcile` from binary print mode; can also be edited by hand.

bound_to.description: Dotted reference into a service's expose_as field. Format: <service>.<field>

startup_tasks.description: Pre-start tasks the binary runs before binding listeners. Auto-populated by `nexus reconcile`.

overrides.description: Reconcile-survival operator patches (legacy). Per-field overrides for the auto-generated services/env sections.

validation.description: Constraints applied to the effective value at boot or pre-render.

enum.description: Allowed values. Effective value must match one exactly.

regex.description: Regular expression the value must match. Re2 syntax (Go-compatible).

min.description: Minimum numeric value (when the field parses as a number).
